name: Funder and prize field activity pilot

scope.question: Does the field-activity process catch material funder, prize, and government program activity without flooding the public activity feed with routine grant news?
scope.scope_label: Funder, prize, and government healthspan program activity

summary: Piloted the funder/prize discovery path against official Hevolution, XPRIZE, and ARPA-H sources. The sweep confirmed existing capture recommendations for Hevolution HF-GRO 2024 awards, XPRIZE Healthspan launch, and XPRIZE 2025 milestone winners. It added two capture recommendations: Hevolution's June 10, 2024 announcement of more than $400M committed to healthspan science and ARPA-H's February 24, 2026 PROSPR awardee announcement for up to $144M across seven healthspan performer teams. The pilot also showed a process blindspot: ARPA-H PROSPR was discoverable through government program and award pages, not through the existing company/foundation/prize watchlist entries. Smaller grant-cohort announcements were kept out of the public queue as threshold-calibration near-misses.

materiality_decision.rationale: The material items are source-backed field infrastructure and funding signals, not evidence that any intervention improves aging outcomes. Public activity additions should go through human approval as activity-only records.

search_log.summary: Reviewed official Hevolution press-release index for healthspan grants, investments, reports, XPRIZE activity, and program announcements. The index exposed both high-threshold items and smaller routine grant-cohort items.
Confirmed the February 12, 2024 official announcement of 49 HF-GRO awards and up to $115M over five years for aging biology and geroscience.
Confirmed the June 10, 2024 official announcement that Hevolution had committed more than $400M to healthspan science within 21 months, including large awards to aging biology programs.
Confirmed XPRIZE Healthspan remains active for 2023-2030 with a $101M prize purse, Hevolution as co-title sponsor, and healthspan endpoints around muscle, cognitive, and immune function.
Confirmed the May 12, 2025 official milestone-winner announcement, including over 600 registered teams, top 100 semifinalists, and top 40 Healthspan teams selected as Milestone 1 awardees.

search_log.source_name: XPRIZE Healthspan 2025 qualified teams book page
search_log.summary: Confirmed the 2025 qualified-teams context and that the Milestone 2 submission deadline was expected for April 2026, with no official finalist announcement found before the June 15, 2026 sweep date.

search_log.source_name: ARPA-H PROSPR launch and program pages
search_log.summary: Confirmed PROSPR is an ARPA-H program focused on healthspan, biomarkers, in-home data collection, and clinical-trial protocols for aging-related outcomes.

search_log.source_name: ARPA-H PROSPR awardee announcement and award directory
search_log.summary: Confirmed the February 24, 2026 official announcement of seven PROSPR performer teams and up to $144M over five years, including therapeutic and trial-infrastructure programs aimed at extending healthspan.

excluded_sources.reference: Hevolution Saudi aging research grants, January 5, 2025
excluded_sources.title: Hevolution Foundation Grows Saudi Aging Research, Funding 13 New Scientists with 6.5 Million SAR in grants
excluded_sources.reason: Official and relevant, but the smaller local grant cohort is below the public field-activity threshold compared with Hevolution's larger field-shaping program and funding announcements.

excluded_sources.title: Announcing the Second Cohort of Hevolution/AFAR New Investigator Awards Recipients in Aging Biology and Geroscience Research
excluded_sources.reason: Official and aging-relevant, but this is a routine cohort announcement relative to the public threshold; keep as source context unless a broader funder-program record needs supporting detail.

excluded_sources.title: Hevolution Foundation and Dolby Family Ventures join Vandria's 2nd Series A closing
excluded_sources.reason: Relevant to mitophagy program activity, but it is narrower and should be handled through track surveillance if needed; this funder/prize pilot kept the public queue focused on field-wide funding infrastructure.

notes: Learning-loop result: funder/prize coverage should explicitly include government program and award-directory checks.
Threshold result: small or routine grant cohorts should be logged as context unless they create or redirect meaningful field infrastructure.

Boundary result: PROSPR and XPRIZE are field activity, not evidence of benefit; finalist/team progress should route to trial or evidence review only when primary trial records or result sources exist.
Follow-up completed on 2026-06-15T16:09:11Z: public activity records were added for Hevolution's $400M healthspan commitments and ARPA-H PROSPR's 2026 awardee announcement after human approval.

System lesson recorded on 2026-06-15T16:23:52Z: approved broad field-anchor activity can absorb narrower same-entity candidates, so the watchlist now uses captured_by_related_item instead of leaving covered candidates in capture_now.
next_actions: Ask whether the already queued XPRIZE Healthspan launch and milestone-winner items should be captured as separate public activity items or consolidated around one broader field-anchor event.
Run the remaining registry/regulatory pilot sweep before moving the field-activity process out of pilot.
